user_query: Add the Miso-Glazed Black Cod with Bok Choy to mothers day 3 dinner

fields.name: Miso-Glazed Black Cod with Sautéed Baby Bok Choy
fields.short_description: Tender black cod brushed with a savory-sweet miso glaze, broiled until caramelized and served with lightly sautéed baby bok choy — elegant, umami-forward, and balanced.
fields.meal_suggestion: Marinate black cod fillets in a mixture of white miso, mirin, sake (or dry white wine), and a touch of brown sugar for at least 30 minutes (or overnight for deeper flavor). Wipe excess marinade, then broil or roast fillets until the glaze is caramelized and fish flakes easily (about 6–8 minutes depending on thickness). Meanwhile, quickly sauté halved baby bok choy in a hot skillet with a splash of sesame oil, minced garlic, and a pinch of salt until tender-crisp. Serve the glazed cod atop or alongside the bok choy, garnished with toasted sesame seeds and thinly sliced scallions. Optional: a small scoop of steamed brown rice or cauliflower rice on the side.

ingredients.name: optional: brown rice or cauliflower rice

fields.nutrition_note: Black cod is rich in heart-healthy omega-3 fatty acids and high-quality protein; miso adds umami and probiotics (if not fully heated) along with sodium—use moderate amounts to control salt. Bok choy provides vitamin C, vitamin K, and fiber with minimal calories. Pairing with whole grain rice adds complex carbohydrates for a balanced meal.

insertion_point.reasoning: Append to Mother's Day 3 Dinner (position -1) to add the requested Miso-Glazed Black Cod option; content focuses on preparation, complementary bok choy side, and nutrition to match meal schema.
